documents.title: NYU Libraries Generative AI and Academic Integrity

ai_strategy.summary: NYU is a major AI research institution with AI@NYU, the Center for Data Science, Center for Responsible AI, the Global AI Frontier Lab with Korea, AI Now, and Empire AI participation. Public academic-integrity guidance is more decentralized and cautious than a single comprehensive university AI policy.

key_provisions: Use AI in accordance with academic integrity expectations and instructor or unit guidance
Do not enter restricted, confidential, regulated, or sensitive data into unapproved AI tools
Review AI outputs for accuracy, hallucination, bias, copyright, and attribution issues
Disclose or cite AI use when required by course, research, publisher, or unit rules
Keep human judgment and accountability in consequential academic, research, and administrative decisions
ai_policy.summary: New York University's AI policy posture is best characterized as cautious: public sources show explicit expectations for responsible use, privacy, disclosure, academic integrity, and human review, while leaving some implementation decisions to units or instructors.

computing_infrastructure.summary: NYU HPC identifies Greene as the university-wide flagship supercomputer and reports 332 high-end RTX8000/V100 GPUs plus 7.3 PB enterprise storage. NYU Langone separately operates BigPurple with 376 GPUs and Skynet with 192 GPUs for clinical and radiology research, so NYU has additional medical-school AI/HPC capacity beyond the central Greene figure.

regulatory_context.summary: New York University operates in New York; public AI guidance should be read alongside applicable state, federal, and institutional privacy, security, accessibility, and academic-integrity requirements.

narrative_summary: New York University (New York, NY) is an R1 institution whose public AI posture shows activity across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tooling, workforce readiness, and access. NYU is a major AI research institution with AI@NYU, the Center for Data Science, Center for Responsible AI, the Global AI Frontier Lab with Korea, AI Now, and Empire AI participation. Public academic-integrity guidance is more decentralized and cautious than a single comprehensive university AI policy. The most concrete public initiatives captured here are AI@NYU coordination hub; Global AI Frontier Lab; NYU participation in Empire AI; NYU Libraries generative AI academic-integrity guidance; AI Now Institute. Across the profile, the strongest evidence comes from official university AI hubs, policy pages, teaching resources, research-center pages, and program pages rather than secondary reporting. The institution_type is set to r1_research from the canonical Carnegie R1 list. Known gaps are intentionally explicit: leadership names, user counts, detailed budgets, and compute counts are only included when they surfaced in public sources. Overall, the profile supports at least five AI initiatives and evidences strategy/leadership, governance, teaching/curriculum, research infrastructure, student services or enterprise tooling, workforce/community, partnerships, and equity dimensions where public sources permit.
